Normal precautions for lithuania (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Lithuania (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Vilnius operations are licensed under the Law on Security Services of the Republic of Lithuania (2004) and multi-point vetted for identity, criminal history, driving competence, and route knowledge of Vilnius. Airport transfers from Vilnius Airport (VNO) use pre-positioned vehicles with routes pre-surveyed to any destination. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in Vilnius.

item.title: Security Drivers in Zagreb, Croatia
item.description:
Security driver services in Zagreb are structured around the low risk environment and the specific operational requirements of Zagreb’s road network, airport, and principal movement corridors. Normal precautions for croatia (2026); US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) for Croatia (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Zagreb operations are licensed under the Private Security Act (2018) and multi-point vetted for identity, criminal history, driving competence, and route knowledge of Zagreb. Airport transfers from Franjo Tudman International Airport (ZAG) use pre-positioned vehicles with routes pre-surveyed to any destination. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in Zagreb.

item.title: Security Drivers in Chisinau, Moldova
item.description:
Security driver services in Chisinau address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Moldovan Transnistria and Russian influence environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; transnistria against all travel; us state dept level 2 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Chisinau operations must demonstrate: current valid Moldova commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Chisinau’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Chisinau International Airport (KIV) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Cotonou, Benin
item.description:
Security driver services in Cotonou address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Benin commercial and port environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; us state dept level 2 overall, level 3 north (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Cotonou operations must demonstrate: current valid Benin commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Cotonou’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Cotonou Cardinal Bernardin Gantin International Airport (COO) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Lome, Togo
item.description:
Security driver services in Lome address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Togo political and Sahel spillover environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; us state dept level 2 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Lome operations must demonstrate: current valid Togo commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Lome’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Lome Tokoin International Airport (LFW) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Malabo, Equatorial Guinea
item.description:
Security driver services in Malabo address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Equatorial Guinea oil sector environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; us state dept level 2 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Malabo operations must demonstrate: current valid Equatorial Guinea commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Malabo’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Malabo International Airport (SSG) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Podgorica, Montenegro
item.description:
Security driver services in Podgorica address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Montenegrin organised crime and inter-clan violence environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution, specifically noting bystander risk from criminal violence; us state dept level 2 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Podgorica operations must demonstrate: current valid Montenegro commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Podgorica’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Podgorica Airport (TGD) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Praia, Cape Verde
item.description:
Security driver services in Praia address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Cape Verde stable Atlantic island environment. Fcdo advises normal precautions; us state dept level 1 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Praia operations must demonstrate: current valid Cape Verde commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Praia’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Nelson Mandela International Airport (RAI) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina
item.description:
Security driver services in Sarajevo address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Bosnian post-conflict and Dayton governance environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution and warns of landmine risk outside urban areas; us state dept level 2 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Sarajevo operations must demonstrate: current valid Bosnia and Herzegovina commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Sarajevo’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Sarajevo International Airport (SJJ) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Skopje, North Macedonia
item.description:
Security driver services in Skopje address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in North Macedonian EU accession and organised crime environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; us state dept level 2 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Skopje operations must demonstrate: current valid North Macedonia commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Skopje’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Skopje Alexander the Great Airport (SKP) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Tirana, Albania
item.description:
Security driver services in Tirana address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Albanian organised crime and EU accession environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; us state dept level 2 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Tirana operations must demonstrate: current valid Albania commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Tirana’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Tirana International Airport Mother Teresa (TIA) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Yerevan, Armenia
item.description:
Security driver services in Yerevan address the transport security requirements of corporate and executive principals operating in Armenian post-conflict geopolitical environment. Fcdo advises a high degree of caution; us state dept level 2, border areas level 3 (2026).

All vetted security drivers for Yerevan operations must demonstrate: current valid Armenia commercial driving licence, criminal record clearance, professional references, and demonstrated knowledge of Yerevan’s road network and security geography. Airport transfers from Zvartnots International Airport (EVN) use pre-arranged vehicles positioned before the principal exits customs. No app-based or informal transport is appropriate for principal movements in this environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Antananarivo
item.description: Antananarivo is the capital and economic hub of Madagascar, a large Indian Ocean island nation with a growing extractive industries sector and a resident diplomatic and development finance community. FCDO advises a high degree of caution throughout Madagascar as of 2026, with the US State Department rating the country at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution). The Ivato International Airport road is a documented robbery and carjacking corridor; inside-terminal collection by a vetted driver is the standard arrival protocol for all corporate visitors.

item.title: Security Drivers in Freetown
item.description: Freetown is the capital and main port of Sierra Leone, a West African nation with one of the world’s most resource-rich subsoils and a security environment shaped by decades of post-conflict recovery. FCDO advises a high degree of caution throughout Sierra Leone as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution). The defining operational feature of Freetown is the Lungi estuary crossing: every arrival and departure requires a planned crossing of the Sierra Leone River, and the mode and timing of that crossing is the primary logistics risk variable.

item.title: Security Drivers in Gaborone
item.description: Gaborone is the capital and commercial hub of Botswana, consistently ranked among sub-Saharan Africa’s most politically stable and well-governed nations. FCDO advises normal precautions for Botswana as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions). The security driver proposition in Gaborone reflects this environment: professional reliability, consistent transfer management, and familiarity with the diamond industry and financial services client profile that defines the city’s corporate visitor base.

item.title: Security Drivers in Maseru
item.description: Maseru is the capital and largest city of Lesotho, a landlocked mountain kingdom entirely enclosed by South Africa. FCDO advises heightened caution in Maseru as of 2026, with the US State Department rating Lesotho at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution). Armed robbery and vehicle crime on the airport road, combined with Lesotho’s history of political instability and security force involvement in domestic politics, make pre-planned, vetted security driver cover an appropriate baseline for corporate visitors.

item.title: Security Drivers in Mbabane
item.description: Mbabane is the administrative capital of Eswatini, a small landlocked kingdom bordering South Africa and Mozambique. FCDO advises normal precautions for Eswatini as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions). The defining logistics feature of security driver operations in Mbabane is the 80 km transfer to King Mswati III International Airport: one of the longest capital-city airport corridors in southern Africa, requiring careful journey-time allocation for all flight connections.

item.title: Security Drivers in Monrovia
item.description: Monrovia is the capital and main port of Liberia, a West African republic with one of the world’s largest open ship registries and significant iron ore and rubber export industries. FCDO advises a high degree of caution throughout Liberia as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution). The defining operational feature of Monrovia is the 60 km Roberts International Airport transfer: a long road through peri-urban and semi-rural terrain where armed robbery and carjacking are documented risks for passengers using informal or unvetted transport.

item.title: Security Drivers in Ouagadougou
item.description: Ouagadougou is the capital of Burkina Faso, a Sahelian state experiencing one of West Africa’s most severe ongoing insurgencies. FCDO advises against all travel to Burkina Faso as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 4 (Do Not Travel). The city has been targeted in multiple high-profile terrorist attacks, most notably the January 2016 Splendid Hotel and Cappuccino Cafe attack (approximately 30 killed) and the March 2018 simultaneous attacks on the French Embassy and General Staff headquarters. Active JNIM and ISGS insurgency, a 2022 military coup, the expulsion of French military forces, and more than 2 million internally displaced persons together place Burkina Faso beyond the scope of standard commercial security driver deployment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Port Louis
item.description: Port Louis is the commercial, governmental, and financial capital of Mauritius, one of Africa and the Indian Ocean’s most stable and prosperous small states. FCDO advises normal precautions for Mauritius as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions). The security driver proposition in Port Louis is built on professional discretion, reliability, and profile-sensitivity rather than threat management: the financial sector and HNWI visitor profile requires transport that is consistently on time, unobtrusive, and operated by drivers who understand confidentiality standards.

item.title: Security Drivers in Victoria, Seychelles
item.description: Victoria is the compact capital of the Seychelles, an archipelago of 115 islands in the Indian Ocean and one of Africa’s most stable and prosperous small states. FCDO advises normal precautions for Seychelles as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions). The security driver proposition in Victoria is centred on professional discretion, logistics reliability, and profile-sensitivity: appropriate standards for the HNWI, private family, and financial sector visitor profile that Seychelles attracts.

item.title: Security Drivers in Windhoek
item.description: Windhoek is the capital and commercial centre of Namibia, one of southern Africa’s most politically stable and well-governed nations. FCDO advises normal precautions for Namibia as of 2026, and the US State Department rates the country at Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions). The defining logistics feature of security driver operations in Windhoek is the 42 km B6 highway transfer to Hosea Kutako International Airport: a long, open road through semi-arid terrain that demands careful journey-time management for all flight connections.

item.title: Security Drivers in Asmara
item.description: Asmara presents a fundamentally different security transport environment from most African capitals. Eritrea does not have an independent commercial private security sector, meaning the security driver services available in comparable African markets are not available here through commercial operators. The primary security concern for international visitors is not street crime but the systematic government surveillance of foreign nationals in one of the continent’s most closed authoritarian states. The US State Department rates Eritrea at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO advises a high degree of caution.

item.title: Security Drivers in Bangui
item.description: Bangui is among the most challenging ground transport environments in Central Africa. The CAR has been in active armed conflict since 2012, with MINUSCA peacekeeping forces, Wagner-affiliated FACA elements, and multiple armed groups operating in and around the capital. FCDO advises against all travel to the CAR, and the US State Department rates it at Level 4 (Do Not Travel) as of 2026. Bangui M’Poko Airport (BGF) is 3 km from the city centre, but even this short transfer requires armoured vehicles, pre-cleared routes, and coordination with MINUSCA and diplomatic security structures.

item.title: Security Drivers in Brazzaville
item.description: Brazzaville’s ground transport environment for international executives is shaped by a high risk rating driven by street crime in the Poto-Poto district and port area, DRC proximity across the Congo River, and an oil-driven economy that draws a significant corporate visitor population. Maya-Maya Airport (BZV) is just 4 km from the city centre, but the transfer is a documented exposure window for informal transport users. Pre-arranged, vetted security drivers operating under Congolese ministerial authorisation provide inside-terminal collection and GPS-tracked movement via the Plateau district corridor.

item.title: Security Drivers in Bujumbura
item.description: Bujumbura is the economic capital of Burundi and the main gateway for visitors to one of Central Africa’s smallest but most densely populated countries. The 2015 coup attempt and subsequent political crisis created a repressive environment that continues to shape the security landscape. The US State Department rates Burundi at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO advises a high degree of caution. Development finance organisations, NGOs, and commercial sector visitors (coffee, tea, telecommunications) form the primary corporate visitor profile.

item.title: Security Drivers in Djibouti City
item.description: Djibouti City occupies a strategic position on the Bab-el-Mandeb strait at the southern entrance to the Red Sea, hosting military bases from the United States, France, China, Japan, and Italy alongside one of East Africa’s most active port complexes. This combination of military logistics, maritime operations, and Horn of Africa regional hub functions creates a specific security driver requirement for corporate visitors. FCDO Djibouti travel advice (2026) notes petty crime and opportunistic theft as the primary concerns, and the US State Department rates Djibouti at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution) as of 2026.

item.title: Security Drivers in Hargeisa
item.description: Hargeisa is the capital of Somaliland, the self-declared independent administration that has maintained relative stability in the northern Somali territories since 1991. Distinct from southern Somalia and Mogadishu in both governance and risk level, Hargeisa supports a functioning development finance, trade, and diplomatic engagement environment. FCDO travel advice (2026) notes Hargeisa’s differentiated security context while advising a high degree of caution, and the US State Department rates Somalia at Level 4 (Do Not Travel) while acknowledging that Somaliland carries different risk characteristics.

item.title: Security Drivers in Juba
item.description: Juba is the capital of South Sudan, one of the world’s youngest and most challenging operating environments for security transport. The country’s oil sector draws corporate visitors, while the UN, NGO, and diplomatic community is large given South Sudan’s ongoing stabilisation requirements. The US State Department rates South Sudan at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO advises a high degree of caution throughout the country. Juba International Airport (JUB) is within the city limits, but even the short transfer requires vetted, pre-arranged transport given the high risk environment.

item.title: Security Drivers in Khartoum
item.description: Khartoum has been a primary theatre of Sudan’s civil war between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) since April 2023. The city’s infrastructure, including Khartoum International Airport, has sustained severe damage. FCDO advises against all travel to Sudan, and the US State Department classifies Sudan at Level 4 (Do Not Travel) as of 2026. Standard security transport services in Khartoum are not available under current conditions, and this page serves as a security context reference for organisations with Sudan interests.

item.title: Security Drivers in Libreville
item.description: Libreville is Gabon’s capital and the commercial hub of an economy driven by oil, manganese mining, and timber. The August 2023 military transition introduced a layer of political unpredictability that warrants structured security planning for corporate visitors. FCDO Gabon travel advice (2026) advises caution and notes crime risk particularly after dark. Oil-sector executives (TotalEnergies, Perenco) and mining sector visitors (Comilog, Eramet) represent the primary corporate traveller profile, and vetted transport from Libreville International Airport (LBV) is the appropriate baseline for any business visit.

item.title: Security Drivers in N'Djamena
item.description: N’Djamena is the capital of Chad, a landlocked Sahelian country whose oil sector (ExxonMobil, CNPC, TotalEnergies) draws corporate visitors and whose large humanitarian sector supports a significant NGO and UN agency presence. Chad operates under a transitional military council following the death of President Idriss Deby in 2021. The US State Department rates Chad at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO advises against all travel to the country’s border regions with Sudan, Libya, Nigeria, and the CAR. In N’Djamena itself, the security environment concentrates on robbery and opportunistic crime risk outside the central diplomatic and commercial districts.

item.title: Security Drivers in Ashgabat
item.description:
Security drivers in Ashgabat are licensed under Turkmenistan’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Ashgabat transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Ashgabat. The primary concerns are heavy state surveillance, movement restrictions outside Ashgabat, and the regulatory constraints of operating in a highly controlled authoritarian environment. Source: FCDO Turkmenistan travel advice (2026).

item.title: Security Drivers in Asuncion
item.description:
Security drivers in Asuncion are licensed under Paraguay’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Asuncion transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Asuncion. The primary concerns are petty crime, bag-snatching, and express kidnapping risk in the informal economy districts. Source: FCDO Paraguay travel advice (2026).

What security drivers cover in Asuncion

Security driver operations in Asuncion cover airport transfers with inside-terminal collection at Silvio Pettirossi International Airport (ASU), in-city movement with pre-planned primary and secondary routes, vehicle discipline protocol throughout all journeys, and operations controller tracking from departure to confirmed arrival.

item.title: Security Drivers in Bishkek
item.description:
Security drivers in Bishkek are licensed under Kyrgyzstan’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Bishkek transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Bishkek. The primary concerns are petty crime, periodic political unrest, and organised criminal networks with documented involvement in cargo theft. Source: FCDO Kyrgyzstan travel advice (2026).

item.title: Security Drivers in Chittagong
item.description:
Security drivers in Chittagong are licensed under Bangladesh’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Chittagong transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Chittagong. The primary concerns are terrorism, political strikes (hartals), and petty crime targeting port and garment-sector visitors. Source: FCDO Bangladesh travel advice (2026).

What security drivers cover in Chittagong

Security driver operations in Chittagong cover airport transfers with inside-terminal collection at Shah Amanat International Airport (CGP), in-city movement with pre-planned primary and secondary routes, vehicle discipline protocol throughout all journeys, and operations controller tracking from departure to confirmed arrival.

item.title: Security Drivers in Dushanbe
item.description:
Security drivers in Dushanbe are licensed under Tajikistan’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Dushanbe transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Dushanbe. The primary concerns are border proximity risks, political sensitivity, and petty crime in market areas. Source: FCDO Tajikistan travel advice (2026).

What security drivers cover in Dushanbe

Security driver operations in Dushanbe cover airport transfers with inside-terminal collection at Dushanbe International Airport (DYU), in-city movement with pre-planned primary and secondary routes, vehicle discipline protocol throughout all journeys, and operations controller tracking from departure to confirmed arrival.

item.title: Security Drivers in Guayaquil
item.description:
Security drivers in Guayaquil are licensed under Ecuador’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Guayaquil transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Guayaquil. The primary concerns are armed robbery, kidnapping for ransom, carjacking, and gang-related violence linked to drug trafficking networks with significant presence in the port and peripheral neighbourhoods. Source: FCDO Ecuador travel advice (2026).

item.title: Security Drivers in La Paz
item.description:
Security drivers in La Paz are licensed under Bolivia’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The La Paz transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to La Paz. The primary concerns are street crime, express kidnapping, political demonstrations that can block major routes, and altitude-related medical risk at 3,640m above sea level. Source: FCDO Bolivia travel advice (2026).

item.title: Security Drivers in Montevideo
item.description:
Security drivers in Montevideo are licensed under Uruguay’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Montevideo transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Montevideo. The primary concerns are petty theft in tourist areas and peripheral neighbourhood crime, both at low levels relative to Latin American regional norms. Source: FCDO Uruguay travel advice (2026).

item.title: Security Drivers in Ulaanbaatar
item.description:
Security drivers in Ulaanbaatar are licensed under Mongolia’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Ulaanbaatar transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Ulaanbaatar. The primary concerns are petty crime including bag-snatching and pickpocketing, drink-spiking in entertainment districts, and extreme winter conditions. Source: FCDO Mongolia travel advice (2026).

item.title: Security Drivers in Vientiane
item.description:
Security drivers in Vientiane are licensed under Laos’s relevant regulatory framework, providing vetted transport for airport transfers and in-city movement with full operations controller support.

The Vientiane transport security environment

A vetted security driver provides significantly better risk management than informal transport for visitors to Vientiane. The primary concerns are petty theft, road accidents, and the regulatory constraints of operating in a one-party state. Source: FCDO Laos travel advice (2026).

item.title: Security Drivers in Belize City
item.description: Belize City presents a concentrated urban security challenge driven by gang territorial activity in south-side neighbourhoods. Belize Police Department statistics (2022) record Belize City’s homicide rate as among the highest in the Caribbean per capita, and FCDO Belize (2026) specifically warns visitors against movement in south-side areas. The US State Department Belize advisory (2026) notes elevated crime risk across the city. For arriving executives, the risk is most acute at Goldson Airport kerbside, where robbery targeting identifiable international arrivals is a documented pattern, and on any route that inadvertently traverses south-side streets rather than maintaining the north-side commercial corridor.

item.title: Security Drivers in Caracas
item.description: Ground movement in Caracas demands a level of planning and vehicle capability that most international business travellers do not associate with a capital city. The combination of express kidnapping risk, colectivo-controlled arterials, carjacking on major roads, and the specific dangers of the Maiquetia airport corridor means that transport in Caracas must be treated as a security function, not a logistics function. A standard taxi or ride-hailing booking is not a safe option for any foreign national in this city.

item.title: Security Drivers in Georgetown
item.description: Georgetown’s security environment has been reshaped by Guyana’s oil boom. ExxonMobil’s Stabroek Block production, which began in 2019, has driven a rapid influx of international business visitors and associated wealth perception, making corporate travellers appear as higher-profile targets for armed robbery. The FCDO advises a high degree of caution for Guyana (2026), and the US State Department rates Guyana at Level 2 (2026). Security drivers in Georgetown address this environment through structured transfer protocols and a 42km airport transfer plan that eliminates the informal-transport vulnerability at Cheddi Jagan International Airport.

item.title: Security Drivers in Guatemala City
item.description: Guatemala City operates in one of Central America’s more demanding transport security environments. The US State Department rates Guatemala at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO Guatemala (2026) specifically documents carjacking and express kidnapping (secuestro express) as risks for vehicle-borne travellers in the capital. Guatemala City’s zone structure means that risk levels vary significantly depending on which zones a journey passes through: Zona 10 and Zona 14 are the lower-risk commercial corridor, whilst peripheral and outer zones carry substantially elevated gang and carjacking risk. La Aurora International Airport sits close to the city but the transfer still requires zone-aware routing to remain within the lower-risk corridor.

item.title: Security Drivers in Kabul
item.description: Ground movement in Kabul under Taliban governance presents a set of challenges that require a fundamentally different approach from conventional high-risk city driving. The threat environment combines IED attacks by IS-KP, Taliban checkpoint authority over all movement, the legacy of the 2021 airport bombing, and a communications infrastructure that can be degraded or suspended at any point. Every journey in Kabul requires a purpose-built plan, not a standard transport booking.

item.title: Security Drivers in Karachi
item.description: Karachi’s scale and complexity make ground movement a substantive security challenge. The city spans hundreds of square kilometres, transit distances between key business and residential districts are significant, and the route options available mean that many journeys cannot avoid passing through or near areas of elevated risk. A professional security driver in Karachi is not a luxury for high-threat environments: it is the appropriate response to the city’s documented kidnap, terrorism, and armed robbery threat.

item.title: Security Drivers in Kathmandu
item.description: Transport in Kathmandu requires a different risk focus from most South Asian cities. The primary concerns are not terrorism or organised crime but rather the practical risks of the airport environment, the unpredictability of bandhs, road safety standards, and the need to maintain operational mobility in a seismically active city. For most international visitors, a pre-booked vetted driver from a registered Nepali operator addresses the core transport security requirement effectively.

item.title: Security Drivers in Kingston
item.description: Kingston presents one of the Caribbean’s more demanding transport security environments. The Jamaica Constabulary Force (JCF) recorded substantial levels of gang-related violence across the capital in 2023, concentrated in inner-city communities but extending to transit routes used by international visitors, particularly those travelling to and from Norman Manley International Airport. The US State Department classifies Jamaica at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and the FCDO (2026) advises against complacency even within the New Kingston business district. Airport transfers represent the highest-risk movement for arriving executives, as the kerbside at KIN is a known observation point for criminal networks targeting international arrivals.

item.title: Security Drivers in Kuwait City
item.description:
Kuwait City’s ground transport environment reflects the broader threat picture that the US State Department rates at Level 3 and FCDO describes with an against-all-but-essential-travel advisory. Hazardous driving conditions are specifically flagged by FCDO documentation, and the operational overlay of Iran-aligned militia activity targeting US military installations means that ground transport is not a routine logistics matter for corporate principals in Kuwait.

Operating environment

The principal safe zones for corporate ground transport in Kuwait City are the Gulf Road corridor, Kuwait City centre, Salmiya and Hawalli. These areas host the majority of international hotels, corporate offices and government ministries relevant to most business itineraries. Ground transport planning specifically avoids Jahra and Jleeb Al Shuyoukh, the districts carrying the highest documented crime risk in the city.

item.title: Security Drivers in Managua
item.description: Managua’s security environment for visiting executives is shaped by Nicaragua’s political landscape as much as by conventional crime risk. The US State Department rates Nicaragua at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO Nicaragua (2026) notes that foreign visitor movements are monitored by state security services and that political demonstrations can arise with limited warning, disrupting road networks across the capital. The primary commercial corridor along Carretera Masaya and the hotel district is the operating base for most corporate visits, but movement outside this corridor requires specific awareness of the political surveillance environment and current demonstration patterns.

item.title: Security Drivers in Panama City
item.description: Panama City’s dual identity as Central America’s primary financial centre and the gateway to one of the world’s most important maritime corridors brings a consistent flow of senior executives, legal professionals and government principals through the city. The ground transport environment reflects the medium risk picture: carjacking and armed robbery are documented on certain corridors, civil unrest is a periodic operational reality, and the Darien province border represents a categorically different risk environment that standard security driving does not cover.

item.title: Security Drivers in Paramaribo
item.description: Paramaribo is a medium-risk environment by regional standards, but the 45km airport transfer on the Johan Adolf Pengel Highway is one of the longest exposed transfer corridors for any capital city in the Americas. The FCDO Suriname advisory (2026) notes robbery risk, particularly after dark, and the US State Department rates Suriname at Level 2 (2026). The combination of a long predictable highway transfer and limited after-dark police presence makes pre-arranged, Ministry of Justice-authorised security driver cover the appropriate baseline for corporate visits rather than relying on kerbside transport at Johan Pengel.

item.title: Security Drivers in Port-au-Prince
item.description: Port-au-Prince is classified by the FCDO (2026) as a destination against all travel, the highest advisory level. The US State Department rates Haiti at Level 4 (Do Not Travel) as of 2026. BINUH reporting for 2024-2025 documents that armed gangs control approximately 85% of the Port-au-Prince metropolitan area, with kidnapping from vehicles, armed roadblocks, and vehicle ambush among the primary tactics used by gang organisations operating in the capital. Toussaint Louverture International Airport has been subject to gang threat and intermittent closure during this period. No movement in Port-au-Prince can be planned without real-time gang activity intelligence, and no vehicle movement is appropriate in a soft-skin vehicle on most routes.

item.title: Security Drivers in San Jose
item.description: San Jose’s position as Costa Rica’s capital and the heart of the country’s technology and medical devices sector brings a consistent flow of multinational executives, investors and senior managers through the city. The overall risk environment is the most favourable in Central America: Costa Rica has maintained political stability since abolishing its standing army in 1948, and the Ministry of Security operates a more developed private security regulatory framework than any neighbouring state.

item.title: Security Drivers in San Salvador
item.description: San Salvador’s security environment has undergone significant transformation since March 2022, when the El Salvador government launched a nationwide security operation under a state of emergency. PNC (National Civil Police) statistics for 2023 document substantial reductions in gang territorial activity and homicide rates across the country, including in the capital. The US State Department rates El Salvador at Level 2 (Exercise Increased Caution) as of 2026, and FCDO El Salvador (2026) continues to advise vigilance, particularly after dark and on the longer transfer routes, reflecting a residual risk environment rather than the acute threat profile of previous years.

item.title: Security Drivers in Santiago
item.description:
Santiago’s ground transport environment is shaped by several specific, FCDO-documented threat patterns that are distinct from the ambient crime picture. The city is assessed as lower risk than most South American capitals, and Chile’s overall stability is genuine. However, the airport taxi scam, tyre puncture scam, vehicle targeting and civil unrest calendar are concrete operational factors that professional security driving directly addresses.

The SCL airport problem

The most immediate risk for corporate arrivals in Santiago is at Arturo Merino Benitez Airport (SCL). FCDO documents individual losses of up to GBP 10,000 from a well-organised taxi scam: criminals in the arrivals hall wear official-looking lanyards and approach passengers before they reach the official pre-paid booth inside the terminal. Fares are then inflated by adding digits to the metre. The mitigation is structural, not behavioural: a pre-briefed officer positioned in the arrivals hall before the principal clears customs, with confirmed named transport to the vehicle. Our SCL collection protocol provides this as standard.

item.title: Security Drivers in Tegucigalpa
item.description: Tegucigalpa operates in one of Central America’s more challenging security environments. The IUDPAS/UNAH violence observatory (2023) documents Honduras among the region’s higher-homicide-rate countries, and Tegucigalpa contributes significantly to national figures driven by gang territorial activity in peripheral colonias. The US State Department rates Honduras at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, and FCDO Honduras (2026) specifically identifies carjacking and kidnapping as risks for vehicle-borne travellers. Toncontin International Airport’s urban location means that airport arrivals exit directly into city traffic with limited buffer, making the transfer protocol one of the most important security decisions of any Tegucigalpa visit.

item.title: Security Drivers in Yaounde
item.description: Yaounde, as Cameroon’s capital and political centre, presents a security environment shaped by the country’s broader instability. The US State Department rates Cameroon at Level 3 (Reconsider Travel) as of 2026, driven in part by the ongoing Anglophone crisis in the North-West and South-West regions, the threat from Boko Haram in the Far North, and general crime risk across the country. FCDO Cameroon (2026) advises against all travel to these three regions. Yaounde itself is not directly affected by these regional conflicts, but the overall advisory level reflects a national environment that warrants structured security planning for corporate visitors, including vetted transport arrangements from Nsimalen International Airport.

item.title: Security Drivers in Bamako
item.description:
Security drivers in Bamako are calibrated to Mali’s specific risk environment: Ministry of Internal Security and Civil Protection-registered operators providing JNIM soft-target awareness for all principal venue transfers, anti-kidnapping movement protocol with no predictable routing and varied departure times, Bamako-Senou Airport inside-terminal collection, no night movement outside the Hamdallaye-ACI 2000 primary zone, and Africa Corps and CNSP checkpoint compliance.

The security driver environment in Bamako

Bamako’s security driver operations are calibrated to Mali’s critical-risk environment: JNIM terrorism awareness, anti-kidnapping movement protocol, junta checkpoint compliance, and the Africa Corps military presence that replaced French Barkhane forces. Only MISPC-licensed operators with current Bamako ground experience are appropriate. Operations controller tracking is maintained throughout every movement, and the medevac protocol for Dakar (approximately 2 hours by air) or Abidjan is a standing element of every Bamako security driver brief.

The security driver environment in Conakry

Guinea’s CNRD junta environment requires security drivers with current checkpoint compliance knowledge and political calendar awareness. Operations controller tracking is maintained throughout every movement, and the medevac protocol for Dakar (approximately 1 hour by air) or Abidjan is a standing element of every Conakry security driver brief.

The security driver environment in Mogadishu

Mogadishu security driver operations are among the most operationally demanding in the world: Al-Shabaab attack capability targeting vehicles, kidnapping risk for foreign nationals, and the absence of reliable state security across the city requiring specialist operators with current Somalia militia landscape knowledge and Halane compound integration. Operations controller tracking is maintained throughout every movement, and the medevac protocol for Nairobi (approximately 1.5 hours by air) is a standing element of every Mogadishu security driver brief.

The security driver environment in Naypyidaw

Naypyidaw operates under Myanmar’s tightest checkpoint and surveillance environment as the military government’s seat. Tripoli security driver operations require current militia landscape knowledge. Route selection is based on real-time militia control information, not historical zone maps.
